experiment: F-NK6 crosslink enforcement prospective validation + NK tracking N=860
prior_work: S420: frontier_crosslink.py built, open_lane.py creation-time suggestion wired. S421: L-940 measured 3.6% linkage. S418: baseline 3.0%.
method: 3-arm measurement: (1) Count lanes opened S420-S424 and check global frontier adoption, (2) NK tracking at N=860, (3) Domain-to-global linkage rate at current state.

crosslink_adoption.note: 1/13 = DOMEX-META-S423-THEOREM targeting F-LEVEL1 directly (not a crosslink adoption). 0/12 adopted crosslink suggestions.

diff_from_expectation: Crosslink adoption LOWER than even the pessimistic prediction (0% strict vs <20%). Linkage DECLINED from 3.6% to 2.5% despite suggestion mechanism — new frontiers created without links dilute the rate. Hub z acceleration extreme (+12.2% in 22 lessons).
novel_finding: L-601 enforcement theorem has two tiers: (1) structural blocking (required fields) achieves near-100% compliance, (2) advisory display (informational suggestions) achieves 0% adoption. Creation-time display is necessary but not sufficient — enforcement must block creation to sustain.

nk_delta_from_s415.interpretation: Graph continuing structural maturity: K_avg decelerating while hub concentration accelerating. L-601 hub now at K_max=161, 4.2x the #2 hub.
